info.name: Exotic Pet Marketplace
info.description:
Full API test suite for the Exotic Pet Marketplace.

🌐 All requests go through the API Gateway on port 8000 ({{GATEWAY_URL}}).
Direct service URLs (8001–8004) are also included for low-level testing.

Gateway route map:
  POST   /api/auth/register          → identity-service
  POST   /api/auth/login             → identity-service
  GET    /api/users/me               → identity-service
  GET    /api/users                  → identity-service (admin)
  PATCH  /api/users/:id/role         → identity-service (admin)
  PATCH  /api/sellers/:id/verify     → identity-service (admin)
  GET    /api/listings               → listing-service
  POST   /api/listings               → listing-service
  GET    /api/listings/:id           → listing-service
  POST   /api/listings/:id/image     → listing-service
  GET    /api/listings/my            → listing-service
  PUT    /api/listings/:id           → listing-service
  DELETE /api/listings/:id           → listing-service
  POST   /api/orders                 → order-service
  GET    /api/orders/my              → order-service
  GET    /api/orders/:id             → order-service
  PATCH  /api/orders/:id/cancel      → order-service
  POST   /api/compliance/check       → compliance-service
  POST   /api/notify/order-confirmed → compliance-service
  GET    /api/audit/logs             → compliance-service
  GET    /api/audit/orders/:id       → compliance-service

Happy-path flow:
1. '🌐 API Gateway' folder → run Register, Login, Verify Seller, Create Listing, Place Order
2. Check Kafka audit logs via gateway to confirm event processing

item.name: 🌐 API Gateway (port 8000)
item.description:
All requests route through the API Gateway on {{GATEWAY_URL}}.
The gateway proxies to the correct microservice and strips the /api prefix.

Run each sub-folder in order for the full happy-path demo.

request.description: Aggregated health check — pings all 4 upstream services and returns their status + latency.
Returns the full routing table: which /api/* prefix maps to which upstream.

item.name: Verify Seller (admin) via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → seller.verified — replace :id

request.description: Set the URL param :id to the seller's user ID (from Get All Users).

item.name: Create Listing (seller) via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → listing.created
Create Listing — Restricted Species via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → listing.created
Delete Listing (seller) via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → listing.deleted
Place Order (buyer) via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → order.placed

request.description:
Places an order through the gateway.

**Kafka event produced:** `order.placed` on `order-events`
**Consumers:** compliance-service (audit + email), listing-service (status update)

item.name: Cancel Order (buyer) via Gateway ⚡ Kafka → order.cancelled

request.description:
Cancels the order.

**Kafka event produced:** `order.cancelled` on `order-events`
**Consumers:** listing-service (reset to available), compliance-service (audit)

item.name: Compliance Check — Restricted species (buyer) → REJECTED via Gateway
Compliance Check — Restricted species (admin) → APPROVED via Gateway
Audit Logs — For Order via Gateway
Audit Logs — Filter by action via Gateway

request.description: Requests without the /api prefix are not routed and return 404.

item.name: Create Listing — Restricted Species (seller) ⚡ Kafka → listing.created

request.description:
Places an order for a listing.

**Kafka events produced:**
- `order.placed` on topic `order-events`

**Consumers:**
- `compliance-service` → writes ORDER_PLACED audit log + sends buyer confirmation email

item.name: Place Order — Restricted Species (buyer) ⚡ Kafka → order.placed (rejected)

request.description:
If the listing species is restricted and the buyer is not admin, the order is saved with status=rejected.

**Kafka events produced:**
- `order.placed` (with complianceAllowed=false) on topic `order-events`

**Consumers:**
- `compliance-service` → writes ORDER_PLACED audit log with rejected status

Cancels a buyer's own order (must have status=created).

**Kafka events produced:**
- `order.cancelled` on topic `order-events`

**Consumers:**
- `listing-service` → resets listing status back to 'available' (direct Supabase update)
- `compliance-service` → writes ORDER_CANCELLED audit log

item.name: GET all logs — filter by action
GET all logs — filter by action REJECTED
GET all logs — filter by order_id
🔔 Kafka Event Verification
item.description:
These requests verify that Kafka events were consumed by compliance-service and written as audit log entries.

Topics & Events:
- order-events  → order.placed, order.cancelled
- user-events   → user.registered, seller.verified
- listing-events → listing.created, listing.deleted

request.description: Verifies the `order.placed` Kafka event was consumed from topic `order-events` and written as an ORDER_PLACED audit log entry.

Verifies the `order.cancelled` Kafka event was consumed from topic `order-events` and written as an ORDER_CANCELLED audit log entry.

Also confirms listing-service consumed the same event to reset listing status to 'available'.

exec: pm.test('Listing status reset to available by Kafka consumer', () => {

request.description: After cancelling an order, confirms the listing-service Kafka consumer (topic: order-events, event: order.cancelled) reset the listing back to 'available'.
Verifies the `user.registered` Kafka event was consumed from topic `user-events` by compliance-service.
Verifies the `seller.verified` Kafka event was consumed from topic `user-events` by compliance-service.
Verifies the `listing.created` Kafka event was consumed from topic `listing-events` by compliance-service.
Verifies the `listing.deleted` Kafka event was consumed from topic `listing-events` by compliance-service.
Shows a count breakdown of all audit actions — use this to confirm how many Kafka events were processed across all topics.
